name: LanceDB
what_it_does: Embedded serverless vector database built on the Lance columnar format, supporting text, image, and multimodal search without a separate server.
use_cases: Build local-first RAG pipelines with no external vector DB infrastructure required
Store and search multimodal embeddings (text + images) in a single table without format conversion
Prototype vector search applications that scale to cloud (LanceDB Cloud) without code changes
Implement hybrid search (vector + full-text + SQL filters) over agent memory stores
Process large embedding datasets with zero-copy Lance format that reads directly from S3/GCS
not_for: High-concurrency production workloads requiring distributed writes without LanceDB Cloud
Teams needing mature managed vector DB with SLA (use Pinecone or Weaviate Cloud)
Applications requiring real-time index updates with sub-millisecond write latency
best_when: Best for local/embedded vector search in agent pipelines where no external infrastructure is acceptable.
avoid_when: Avoid for high-write-throughput production workloads without LanceDB Cloud's managed offering.

auth.notes: Embedded library — no auth for local. LanceDB Cloud uses API key auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully open source for local use
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 licensed for self-hosted. LanceDB Cloud has separate pricing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors from underlying Lance format can be cryptic Rust panics — check version compatibility first.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Inserts are append-only by default — use merge_insert for upsert semantics to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: LanceDB does not deduplicate on insert — calling table.add() twice with same data creates duplicates; use merge_insert() for upserts
ANN index (IVF_PQ) must be created explicitly after bulk ingestion — queries without index do brute-force scan which is slow for >100K vectors
Index is not incrementally updated on new inserts — rebuild index periodically or use optimize() to compact and reindex
The Python and JavaScript SDKs have slightly different APIs — documentation examples may be for the wrong language binding
Remote storage (S3/GCS) connectivity requires credentials in environment — no explicit auth parameter in LanceDB itself

security_score_components.security_notes: No built-in access control for local deployment — rely on filesystem permissions. LanceDB Cloud uses API key auth.
